textPlain: I think Optimize-VHD will do the same thing as messing with diskpart.It's a shame you can't just turn on live TRIM support.This kind of disk image is a bunch of multi-megabyte blocks of data, plus a list of where each block goes on the virtual disk.  Implementations can support TRIM by deleting the block that's zeroed and moving the block at the end of the file into that spot.  VirtualBox can do this, shrinking the file when the guest OS TRIMs, but Hyper-V can't.
